00029 
00030 #include <errno.h>
00031 #include <stdlib.h>
00032 #include <unistd.h>
00033 #include <string.h>
00034 
00035 /*
00036  * system(): ANSI C
00037  *
00038  * Run a command.
00039  */
00040 
00041 #define MAXCMDSIZE 2048
00042 #define MAXARGS    128
00043 
00044 int
00045 system(const char *cmd)
00046 {
00047         /*
00048          * Ordinarily, you call the shell to process the command.
00049          * But we don't know that the shell can do that. So, do it
00050          * ourselves.
00051          */
00052 
00053         char tmp[MAXCMDSIZE];
00054         char *argv[MAXARGS+1];
00055         int nargs=0;
00056         char *s;
00057         int pid, status;
00058 
00059         if (strlen(cmd) >= sizeof(tmp)) {
00060                 errno = E2BIG;
00061                 return -1;
00062         }
00063         strcpy(tmp, cmd);
00064 
00065         for (s = strtok(tmp, " \t"); s; s = strtok(NULL, " \t")) {
00066                 if (nargs < MAXARGS) {
00067                         argv[nargs++] = s;
00068                 }
00069                 else {
00070                         errno = E2BIG;
00071                         return 1;
00072                 }
00073         }
00074 
00075         argv[nargs] = NULL;
00076 
00077         pid = fork();
00078         switch (pid) {
00079             case -1:
00080                 return -1;
00081             case 0:
00082                 /* child */
00083                 execv(argv[0], argv);
00084                 /* exec only returns if it fails */
00085                 _exit(255);
00086             default:
00087                 /* parent */
00088                 waitpid(pid, &status, 0);
00089                 return status;
00090         }
00091 }
